name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
description: An ancient rock fortress and palace ruin rising 200 meters from the surrounding plain. Built during the reign of King Kashyapa I in the 5th century AD, Sigiriya is a UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring frescoes, mirror walls, and the iconic Lion Rock gateway.
thingsToDo: Bird watching in the surrounding forest

name: Ella
description: A small hill town nestled in the mountains of Sri Lanka's Uva Province, surrounded by lush tea estates and spectacular valleys. Famous for the Nine Arch Bridge, Little Adam's Peak, and Ella Rock, it is a paradise for trekkers and nature lovers.

name: Kandy
description: The cultural capital of Sri Lanka and home to the famous Temple of the Tooth Relic (Sri Dalada Maligawa). Surrounded by lush green hills and a scenic lake, Kandy was the last capital of the ancient kings and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
thingsToDo: Explore the Royal Botanical Gardens in Peradeniya
entryFee: LKR 1500 for Temple of the Tooth

name: Yala National Park
description: Sri Lanka's most visited and second largest national park, famous for having one of the world's highest densities of leopards. Yala also hosts elephants, sloth bears, crocodiles, and hundreds of bird species across its diverse ecosystems of jungle, lagoons, and coastline.
entryFee: USD 25 per person + USD 30 per jeep

name: Galle Fort
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and the best-preserved sea fort in Asia, originally built by the Portuguese in 1588 and reinforced by the Dutch in the 17th century. The old city of Galle within the fort walls is a labyrinth of colonial streets, boutique hotels, and galleries.

name: Nuwara Eliya
description: Known as 'Little England', Nuwara Eliya sits at 1,868 meters above sea level amid Sri Lanka's central highlands. Famous for its colonial architecture, sprawling tea estates, cool climate, and the beautiful Horton Plains National Park with its dramatic World's End cliff drop.

name: Mirissa Beach
description: One of Sri Lanka's most beautiful beaches with a crescent-shaped bay, coconut trees, and clear blue waters. Mirissa is the best spot in the country for whale watching, with blue whales and sperm whales frequently sighted offshore between November and April.

name: Anuradhapura
description: One of the ancient capitals of Sri Lanka and a sacred city for Buddhists worldwide. Home to some of the oldest documented trees in human history including the Jaya Sri Maha Bodhi — a sacred fig tree grown from a cutting of the original tree under which the Buddha attained enlightenment.
